steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is D, $f(x)=48(4,096)^{\frac{x}{2}}$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: Every increase of 2 in $a$ multiplies $f(a)$ by the constant $c$. All four choices are equivalent forms of the same function; verify with the remaining alternative: $f(x)=48(64)^{x}$. When $x$ increases by 2, $f(x+2)=48(64)^{x+2}=48(64)^x \cdot 64^2 = f(x)\cdot 4096$. So $c=4096$.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: The question asks which form displays $c$ as the base or coefficient. Since $c=4096$, we need a form where 4096 appears literally as the base of the exponential or as a coefficient.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content:
Check each choice for whether its base or coefficient equals 4096:
  • A: $f(x)=48(2)^{6x}$ — base is 2, not 4096. When $x$ increases by 2, factor is $2^{12}=4096$, but 4096 is not visible as the base or coefficient. Eliminated.
  • B: $f(x)=48(8)^{2x}$ — base is 8, not 4096. Factor per $+2$ is $8^4=4096$, but 4096 is not displayed. Eliminated.
  • $f(x)=48(64)^{x}$: $f(x)=48(64)^x$ — base is 64, not 4096. Factor per $+2$ is $64^2=4096$, but 4096 is not displayed. Eliminated.
  • $f(x)=48(4,096)^{\frac{x}{2}}$: $f(x)=48(4096)^{\frac{x}{2}}$ — base is 4096. When $x$ increases by 2, $\frac{x}{2}$ increases by 1, so $f$ multiplies by $4096^1=4096=c$. The value of $c$ is the base itself. That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
